sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current state after SPACE
seg = current_frame.segmentation
colors = {}
for n in seg['nodes']:
    c = n['color']
    colors[c] = colors.get(c, 0) + n['pixels']
print(f"Current colors: {colors}")

# Check character position
white_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 50]
for n in white_nodes:
    print(f"Character: pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Check ascii around character
lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nBoard (rows 28-50, cols 33-52):")
for i in range(28, 50):
    print(f"{i:2d}: {lines[i][33:52]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Current state: R=36, G=0, O=108, character vertical at rows 38-58, cols 38-46
# Let me try SPACE to rotate and see the pattern
# I'll cycle through all orientations and track R, G, O

states = []
for i in range(4):
    seg = current_frame.segmentation
    colors = {}
    for n in seg['nodes']:
        c = n['color']
        colors[c] = colors.get(c, 0) + n['pixels']
    white_nodes = [n for n in seg['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'W' and n['pixels'] > 50]
    char_pos = white_nodes[0]['boundary'] if white_nodes else None
    states.append((i, colors.get('R',0), colors.get('G',0), colors.get('O',0), char_pos))
    if i < 3:
        action(['SPACE'])

print("Orientation states:")
for i, r, g, o, pos in states:
    print(f"  Orientation {i}: R={r}, G={g}, O={o}, char={pos}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
